ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the employee */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the employee */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee was hired */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee was terminated (if applicable) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current employment status (e.g., Active, Terminated) */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's role */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the employee belongs to */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location of the employee */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's direct manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "parent_department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent department (for hierarchical structure) */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location of the department */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager overseeing the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Budget allocated to the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_parent_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the role belongs to */,
  "grade" TEXT /* Grade or level of the role */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_roles_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the location */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the location */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person for the location */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being reviewed */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Performance rating (e.g., 1-5 scale) */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date of the performance review */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments from the reviewer */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reviewer */,
  "reviewer_comments" TEXT /* Additional comments from the reviewer */,
  "reviewer_rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the reviewer */,
  "self_rating" INTEGER /* Self-rating of the employee */,
  "self_comments" TEXT /* Self-comments of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "promotions" (
  "promotion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each promotion */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being promoted */,
  "previous_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's previous role */,
  "new_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's new role */,
  "promotion_date" TEXT /* Date of the promotion */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the promotion */,
  "promotion_type" TEXT /* Type of promotion (e.g., role change, salary increase) */,
  "new_salary" TEXT /* New salary after the promotion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("promotion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotions_previous_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("previous_role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotions_new_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("new_role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotions_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "onboarding" (
  "onboarding_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each onboarding record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the new employee */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the onboarding process started */,
  "completion_date" TEXT /* Date when the onboarding process was completed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the onboarding process */,
  "training_completed" INTEGER /* Indicates if the onboarding training is completed */,
  "trainer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the trainer */,
  "training_session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the training session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("onboarding_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_onboarding_trainer_id FOREIGN KEY ("trainer_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_onboarding_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_onboarding_training_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("training_session_id") REFERENCES training ("training_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "offboarding" (
  "offboarding_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each offboarding record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee leaving */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee's last day is */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for leaving */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the offboarding process */,
  "exit_interview_date" TEXT /* Date of the exit interview */,
  "exit_interview_comments" TEXT /* Comments from the exit interview */,
  "final_salary" TEXT /* Final salary paid to the employee */,
  "benefits_status" TEXT /* Status of the employee's benefits */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("offboarding_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_offboarding_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "training" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training session */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee attending the training */,
  "training_name" TEXT /* Name of the training session */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the training session */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the training session */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the training session */,
  "trainer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the trainer */,
  "training_cost" TEXT /* Cost of the training session */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Certifications earned from the training */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_training_trainer_id FOREIGN KEY ("trainer_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_training_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role to which the permission is assigned */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "scope" TEXT /* Scope of the permission (e.g., global, departmental) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read, write, execute) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_hierarchy" (
  "hierarchy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role hierarchy record */,
  "parent_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent role */,
  "child_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the child role */,
  "level" INTEGER /* Level of the role in the hierarchy */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role hierarchy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hierarchy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_hierarchy_parent_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_hierarchy_child_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("child_role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_clearances" (
  "clearance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security clearance */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee with the clearance */,
  "clearance_level" TEXT /* Level of the security clearance */,
  "issue_date" TEXT /* Date when the clearance was issued */,
  "expiry_date" TEXT /* Date when the clearance expires */,
  "renewal_date" TEXT /* Date when the clearance needs to be renewed */,
  "issuing_authority" TEXT /* Name of the issuing authority */,
  "clearance_type" TEXT /* Type of clearance (e.g., government, internal) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("clearance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_clearances_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
